finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_03.md:81; chapters/chapter_03.md:91; chapters/chapter_03.md:93; chapters/chapter_03.md:95; chapters/chapter_03.md:97; chapters/chapter_03.md:107; chapters/chapter_03.md:111
finding_results.notes: Reworked the lunch dialogue at lines 81-111. Doug now self-corrects the gallon math mid-sentence (line 81: 'Well, the pool holds about eight thousand full, but February in central Ohio, snowmelt, three-quarter capacity, so call it six thousand'), which is a believable derailment matching his factual self-correction habit. Roy's responses now redirect rather than prompt: 'Whose policy covers it' (line 91) compresses ahead to the central question instead of narrating back what Doug said, and 'I don't have a pool' (line 95) takes the negligence discussion from abstract policy argument into practical personal territory, forcing Doug to reframe ('Right, but if you did. Would you go out there every fall and check the seams?') and Roy's flat 'No' (line 99) resolves the negligence question through demonstration rather than exposition. Food interrupts the briefing cadence: Doug sets his sandwich down at line 93 because he is too engaged with the claim to eat, and Roy takes another bite at line 91. Restored snapshot spoken texture: 'The neighbor has roses' / 'They're all dead' (line 85/89), 'Either one probably would've done it' (line 89), 'The roses?' framing (line 107), cultivar prices in dialogue (line 107). Doug's speech now sounds like a man thinking through a problem over lunch rather than presenting a memo.

summary: Revised the Doug-Roy lunch dialogue (lines 81-111) to fix the single finding. Added a factual self-correction derailment in Doug's speech (gallon math) and replaced Roy's prompt-style responses with redirecting ones — 'Whose policy covers it' compresses ahead, and the 'I don't have a pool' exchange turns the negligence argument personal, changing the conversation's shape. Restored spoken texture from the pre-revision snapshot (cultivar prices, the 'roses?' framing, 'they're all dead' flatness). Physical actions (sandwich set down, bites taken) break the briefing cadence. All other chapter dialogue was checked for register compliance and found sound — no additional changes needed.
